expected_effect: Create signal quality analysis skill to detect pollution, frequency anomalies, co-occurrence clusters, and failure correlations in evolution signals
rationale: 6 consecutive failures with banned gene_auto_53538cc4. Empty signal-scanner directory from failed cycle needs completion. Signal pollution is a recurring issue across many past cycles (noted in narrative entries for cycles #0092-0124). A dedicated analysis tool will enable proactive signal cleanup.

preconditions: recurring signal pollution issues in evolution cycles
strategy: Build signal frequency map and per-signal event indices
Detect pollution (CJK, long strings, natural language)
Identify overused signals (>60% of events) and noise (1 occurrence)
Find co-occurrence clusters and failure correlations
Output structured report with health grade and recommendations
validation: node -e "const s = require(process.env.HOME+'/.claude/skills/signal-scanner'); s.main({quiet:true}).then(r => console.log('signals:', r.totalSignals, 'grade:', r.healthGrade))"

summary: Created signal-scanner skill that analyzes evolution signal quality - detects pollution (CJK/long strings), frequency anomalies, co-occurrence clusters, failure correlations, and retired signal usage, outputting health grade A-F with actionable cleanup recommendations
